detail: Framed 'why does biological aging occur' as a search for one upstream cause generating the hallmarks, explaining both universality and >100-fold rate variation

Set requirements: one-sentence principle; explain universality+rate variation; order hallmarks; differential vs 7 theories; falsifiable experiment; quantitative grounding; every claim connector-verified
Considered signal-level (hyperfunction), resource-amount (disposable soma), and control-reference-structure; chose 'developmental reference but no encoded adult set-point' as the new causal variable
Searched 'set-point/control-theory/quasi-programmed/developmental theory of aging' to test novelty
evidence: PubMed; found Blagosklonny, de Magalhaes 22964300, Kirkwood 18318658, developmental-drift 27490570 as antecedents

detail: Verified hallmarks, hyperfunction, epigenetic-information, rapamycin, clock, disposable-soma, free-radical, naked mole-rat by matching returned title/year/journal
Pulled AnAge (HAGR) build 14 after requesting network access to genomics.senescence.info; 4,645 species, 31 life-history columns
Confirmed 12 controller-protein accessions at the protein level; MTOR=P42345 (2549 aa)
Confirmed controller genes form one insulin/IGF->AKT->mTOR/autophagy/FOXO axis
evidence: Reactome v97: MTOR->MTOR signalling/Macroautophagy; IGF1R->IGF1R cascade; FOXO/AKT

detail: Confirmed controller network expressed in all 54 human tissues >1 TPM (universality support)
evidence: GTEx gtex_v8: MTOR 54/54 median 14.1 TPM; AKT1 50.2; FOXO3 29.8

detail: Retrieved controller disease associations; top hits are growth/body-size phenotypes
evidence: Open Targets: GHR ENSG00000112964->Laron 0.83; MTOR->overgrowth; IGF1R->growth delay; INSR->growth-resistance

detail: Confirmed rapamycin/metformin act on growth controller; senolytics on distinct targets
evidence: ChEMBL: rapamycin CHEMBL413->CHEMBL1902 (FKBP1A/mTOR); metformin CHEMBL1431->complex I; dasatinib CHEMBL1421->ABL; navitoclax CHEMBL443684->BCL-2

detail: Located mouse aging atlas and human aging series for the experiment readout
evidence: GEO: GSE132040 (Tabula Muris Senis bulk, n=947); 215 human aging series; 12 rapamycin; 41 CR

detail: Pulled exact rapamycin lifespan numbers from abstracts
evidence: Harrison 2009 (19587680): +14% F/+9% M; Miller 2014 (24341993, doi:10.1111/acel.12194): +23% M/+26% F at 3x dose, distinct from DR

detail: Confirmed GHR-KO abolishes DR's further lifespan benefit (non-additive -> one pathway)
Computed max-lifespan fold-range on quality-filtered AnAge records
Fitted lifespan vs body-mass allometry; mass explains ~50% of variance
Tested developmental tempo vs lifespan/aging-rate controlling body mass (log-log partial correlation, Fisher-z CIs), using lifespan-independent proxies to avoid circularity
evidence: mammal maturity r=+0.65[+0.61,+0.69] n=801; growth-rate r=-0.37 n=215; MRDT-vs-maturity r=+0.85[+0.62,+0.94] n=17; bird maturity r=+0.36 n=807

detail: Reported that the bird growth-rate signal vanishes after mass correction (honest null, kept as limitation)
Aggregated to order level to partly address phylogenetic non-independence
Quantified naked-mole-rat-vs-mouse dissociation
evidence: AnAge: 1.7x mass, 5.4x maturity, 7.8x lifespan; NMR lives 3.7x longer than mass predicts

detail: Pre-registered STRING betweenness + 10,000x permutation null asking if nutrient-sensing nodes are more central; result was null
evidence: NS 0.0147 vs other 0.0177 (ratio 0.83), p=0.45; top hub TP53

detail: Dropped any 'nutrient-sensing is topologically upstream' claim; rested controller primacy on intervention/genetics/comparative evidence instead
Built Figure 1 (mass-corrected tempo vs aging rate, 3 panels) with 95% CIs; used strict complete-case partial correlation so plotted r matches ledger
Built Figure 2 (allometry residuals + NMR-vs-mouse lollipop)
Built Figure 3 ordering the hallmarks: root set-point -> commission/omission arms -> generated vs permitted hallmarks -> aging
Built Figure 4 differential vs the 7 theories with cited attributions
Re-checked all 19 cited PMIDs by matching title/year/journal; fixed a metadata parser key bug (pmid vs pubmed) that had spuriously shown empties
Corrected daf-2 attribution to Kenyon 1993
evidence: PMID 8247153, Nature, doi:10.1038/366461a0 'A C. elegans mutant that lives twice as long as wild type'

detail: Verified two foundational papers not indexed in PubMed via Crossref
evidence: Williams 1957 doi:10.1111/j.1558-5646.1957.tb02911.x; Medawar 1952 flagged (reprint doi:10.4324/9780429299759-3, no native DOI for original)

detail: Removed memorized DR '~40-50%' after finding DR magnitude is genotype-dependent and can shorten life; kept DR qualitative
evidence: PMID 24637399 'The fine line between lifespan extension and shortening in response to caloric restriction'

detail: Verified telomere-attrition primary source so every theory in the differential table has ledger backing
Repositioned the mouse/NMR mass annotation in Figure 2b into clear whitespace (dashed reference line had occluded the '20 g' as '10 g')
evidence: fig2 v2; annotation 'mouse 20 g · NMR 35 g'

detail: Corrected Figure 4 caption to state attributions verified via PubMed and (for Williams/Medawar) Crossref; added telomere author/year
Wrote result.md, reasoning.md, sources.md, process_trace.json; kept the four figures; ledger 47 entries (35 CITED, 12 INFERENCE)
